reason: The statement applies because the text describes a situation where King Nebuchadnezzar set up a golden image and commanded everyone to worship it. Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, three Jews, refused to worship the idol and only worshipped their own God, showing their dedication to monotheism and rejection of idolatry. This act of defiance against idol worship led them to be thrown into a fiery furnace, but they were miraculously saved by their God. The text does not explicitly mention a prophet warning of impending disaster because of idolatry, but the narrative clearly illustrates the consequences of refusing to worship idols in favor of serving the true God.
